title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Inflamed Gums
intro_desc: If you're struggling with inflamed gums, herbal essential oils can be a big help. These oils are made from plants and have special properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Herbal teas can also relieve inflamed gums, and this is because they help to calm the body and fight off infection.  One of the main reasons herbal teas are so effective is that they're rich in antioxidants, which are like superheroes that save our cells from damage. When we drink herbal tea, these antioxidants get into our bloodstream and start fixing the damage that's causing our gums to become inflamed.  Some herbs that are great for inflamed gums are Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Salvia officinalis. Eucalyptus globulus has a cooling effect that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that can fight off infection and promote healing. Salvia officinalis, or sage, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain.  When we use these herbs to relieve inflamed gums, we can experience some amazing benefits. For one, we can get rid of the pain and discomfort that comes with inflamed gums. We can also prevent more serious problems like gum disease and tooth loss. And because these herbs are natural, we don't have to worry about any harsh chemicals or side effects.  So, if you're struggling with inflamed gums, give herbal essential oils and teas a try. They're a safe and effective way to get relief from pain and discomfort, and they can even help to prevent more serious problems down the line. Just remember to always use high-quality oils and teas, and to follow the instructions carefully.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like cineole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums. The oil's antimicrobial properties, thanks to the presence of compounds like alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, fight bacteria that can cause gum infections. Cineole's ability to increase blood flow to the affected area also helps to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the oil's analgesic properties, due to the presence of eucalyptol, provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps to promote a healthy oral environment and alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water on low heat and add the Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Let it simmer for 2-3 hours to release the oil from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the oil from the solids.
Store the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains a compound called cineole, which has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the bacteria that cause gum inflammation. Cineole also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness in the gums. Additionally, the oil contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that contribute to gum disease. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils work together to help soothe and calm inflamed gums, promoting healthy healing and reducing discomfort. When applied topically, these compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for gum tissue to recover.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves. Choose leaves with no signs of mold or damage.
Wash the leaves with 1 cup of cold water to remove dirt and bacteria. Pat them dry with a paper towel.
Place the leaves in a clean glass jar and cover them with 2 cups of 100 proof vodka. Make sure the leaves are completely covered.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a dark place, shaking the jar every day. This will help extract the oil from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container. Discard the solids and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid, tannins, and ursolic acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums. The essential oil also contains antioxidant properties that prevent damage to gum tissue and promote healing. The antibacterial properties of salvia officinalis essential oil help fight off infection and prevent further inflammation. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, salvia officinalis essential oils can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water.
Wash the leaves with clean water and let them dry with a paper towel.
In a saucepan, combine the dried leaves and water. Heat it over low heat for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a cool, dark place. Use a few drops of the oil to rub on inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is a potent anti-inflammatory agent. Gingerol blocks the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. Another compound called shogaol, also found in Zingiber officinale essential oils, has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections in inflamed gums. The anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of gingerol and shogaol work together to reduce swelling and prevent further irritation. By applying Zingiber officinale essential oils to the gums, you can help alleviate inflammation and promote a healthy environment for healing.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Zingiber officinale roots, a still or distillation apparatus, and a glass bottle.
Wash the roots with 2 cups of water and let them dry.
Place the roots in the still and add 1 cup of water. Heat the mixture until steam rises.
Collect the steam and direct it into the glass bottle. Allow the steam to condense into oil.
Filter the oil using a coffee filter and store it in the glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol and carvacrol, two powerful bioactive constituents. Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help fight off bacteria causing gum inflammation, while carvacrol reduces swelling and eases pain. Thymol also has anti-inflammatory properties that minimize tissue damage and promote healing. The antioxidant properties of carvacrol protect gums from oxidative stress and promote a healthy environment for healing. By reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and promoting healing, Thymus vulgaris essential oils can help alleviate inflamed gums.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of water.
Add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to a saucepan and pour in the water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Let the mixture cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains carnosic acid, a potent antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation. This oil also contains 1,8-cineole, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of carnosic acid can help soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of carnosic acid can help protect the gums from further damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and combating bacteria, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help alleviate gum inflammation and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil, and a clean glass bottle.
Wash the rosemary leaves with water and pat dry with a paper towel. Use a blender or food processor to chop the leaves into small pieces.
Combine the chopped rosemary leaves and carrier oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat (100°F - 150°F) for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids. Add the vitamin E oil and mix well.
Label and store the rosemary essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops of the oil per application for inflamed gums, mixed with water to create a mouthwash.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, caffeic acid, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce swelling and fight off infections in the gums. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea essential oils can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which are often responsible for inflamed gums. Caffeic acid has antioxidant properties that can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, while flavonoids can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. By using Echinacea purpurea essential oils, you can potentially reduce the severity of inflamed gums and promote a healthy mouth environment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers and 2 cups of water.
Combine flowers and water in a saucepan and heat on low.
Let it simmer for 10 minutes, then turn off the heat and let cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discard solids.
Use a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to dilute the liquid to 10% essential oil concentration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils contains a compound called Eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums. Eugenol also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause gum inflammation. The essential oil also contains Cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from further damage. These properties, combined with the essential oil's ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, make it a useful natural remedy for inflamed gums. Regular use of Syzygium aromaticum essential oils may help to reduce gum inflammation and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedies.image_alt: Syzygium aromaticum herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Syzygium aromaticum (Cloves) and a cup of carrier oil like Coconut or Olive oil.
Grind 2 tablespoons of Syzygium aromaticum in a blender or mortar and pestle until it's a fine powder.
Combine the powder with a cup of carrier oil in a clean glass bottle. Shake well for 2 minutes.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the bottle every day. This allows the oil to infuse.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and use the essential oil as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene and geraniol, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. The antiseptic properties of the oil, due to the presence of alpha-pinene, help to prevent infection and promote healing. Cymbopogon citratus essential oils also has antimicrobial properties, which help to kill bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils can help to promote healthy gums and prevent further inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems. Rinse them with water.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to heat the leaves and stems. Add 2 cups of water to the apparatus.
Collect the steam and direct it into a condenser. The condenser should be connected to a collection vessel.
Collect the essential oil as it condenses in the collection vessel. You may need to filter it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Store the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a mouthwash to soothe inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a high concentration of bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight free radicals in the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcuma longa essential oils can help to ease inflammation in the gums, reducing redness and swelling. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of enzymes that cause inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation. Regular use of curcuma longa essential oils may help to promote healthy gums and reduce the risk of gum disease.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Curcuma longa roots and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Chop the dried Curcuma longa roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of carrier oil over the chopped roots, making sure they are completely covered.
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the roots.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Curcuma longa essential oil is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains bioactive constituents like glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps reduce swelling in inflamed gums, making it easier to manage conditions like gingivitis. Glycyrrhizin's ability to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation, also contributes to its anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils, such as glabridin and licoflavonone, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect gum tissue from damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting gum health, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can help soothe inflamed gums and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 cup of the root powder with 2 cups of the carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This allows the roots to infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container. Discard the solids and use the essential oil for inflamed gums.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce inflammation in the gums. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the gums. These teas can also help reduce bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation. Regular consumption may help prevent inflamed gums from forming.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for inflamed gums daily can be okay, but it depends on the tea. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, may help soothe and calm your gums. However, others like sage and eucalyptus can be too strong and cause more irritation if used too frequently.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce inflamed gums, but it takes some time to show results. It may start to feel a bit better within a few days to a week. As you continue to drink the tea, your gums may become less red, swollen, and sore. It's usually a gradual process that happens over a period of several weeks.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for inflamed gums can be most soothing right before bed. The calmness of the evening can help your body relax and reduce swelling. The gentle warmth of the tea can also ease any discomfort. As you drift off to sleep, your gums may feel better by morning.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for inflamed gums. Some herbs like ginger and turmeric can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding. This is a concern if you're taking medications that also thin the blood, like aspirin. It's also possible that certain herbs can affect how medications work, but more research is needed to understand these effects.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can affect the baby's health. If you have inflamed gums, try a simple, sugar-free tea like peppermint or chamomile. These teas can help with pain and discomfort, but check the ingredients first to ensure they're safe for you and your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's generally safe to drink herbal teas for inflamed gums. However, check the ingredients first. Some herbal teas, like chamomile, can be soothing, while others, like sage, might not be suitable for breastfeeding mothers. It's also a good idea to choose tea bags or loose-leaf tea from reputable sources to avoid any potential contaminants.
